preview_text:
The statistical model favors Australia marginally, assigning a 35% win probability against the USA's 34%, a near-even split. Polymarket traders align much more closely with the USA, pricing American victory at 61% and Australia at just 18%, a gap of 43 percentage points separating the model's view from the betting market's.

The model's forecast reflects a tightly competitive encounter. Draw probability sits at 32%, suggesting genuine uncertainty about a decisive outcome. The three outcomes cluster within a narrow range, indicating the teams are similarly matched in the model's assessment and any result falls comfortably within the realm of plausibility.

A substantial divergence separates the model and market consensus. Sportsbook aggregate (stripped of vigorish) and Polymarket both lean heavily toward USA victory—59–61% versus the model's 34%—while both price Australia's win chances at roughly half what the model estimates. The dashboard makes no claim to resolve which assessment is more sound; the gap reflects meaningful disagreement about relative strength.
